ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general iced tea class; no specific oolong lemonade blend class exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EJX covers ready-to-drink tea broadly; no specific oolong lemonade RTD code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34111008 covers tea as a substance; no specific oolong lemonade beverage concept exists. Closest parent used.

preparation_method: blended with lemon juice or lemonade

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard tall-size serving of approximately 355 ml (12 fl oz), as typically served at Starbucks Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks Iced Shaken Oolong Tea Lemonade — Nutrition Information; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central: Lemonade and Oolong Tea component data
nutrient_source.organization: Starbucks Corporation (official nutrition disclosure) / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Starbucks Singapore does not publish full per-100g nutrient panels publicly. Nutrient values are estimated by combining Starbucks US nutrition data for Iced Shaken Oolong Tea Lemonade (Tall, no classic syrup variant and standard variant) with USDA FoodData Central data for brewed oolong tea (FDC ID 171917) and lemonade (FDC ID 167741), weighted to approximate a 355 ml serving composition of approximately 60% oolong tea and 40% lemonade with added sugar syrup. Values are expressed per 100 g (assuming density ≈ 1.0 g/ml for this dilute beverage). This is an estimated record; confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — lemonade and sweetened tea analogues; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Starbucks Iced Shaken Oolong Lemonade. GI estimated at 40 based on analogous lightly sweetened iced tea with lemon (GI ~35–45 range per University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened tea beverages). Oolong tea itself has negligible GI; the lemonade/sugar component drives the GI. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 19.5 g carbs in 355 ml serving) / 100 ≈ 7.8; rounded to 10.8 using 27 g carbs in full standard Starbucks tall serving. Estimation basis documented per Veritas policy.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~14 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 14 mg sodium per 355 ml serving, this beverage poses minimal sodium load and is generally suitable for individuals managing hypertension, provided no additional syrups or salt-containing modifiers are added.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar from lemonade and classic syrup contributing approximately 18–19 g of free sugars per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this beverage as the lemonade and optional classic syrup contribute significant free sugars (~18–19 g per tall serving), which can elevate postprandial blood glucose; requesting the unsweetened or reduced-syrup version is advisable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; oolong tea polyphenols may offer mild cardioprotective benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This beverage contains negligible saturated fat and cholesterol, and oolong tea polyphenols have been associated with modest improvements in lipid profiles in observational studies; however, the added sugar content should be moderated in a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Shaken Oolong Tea Lemonade is a cold beverage sold at Starbucks Singapore under the proprietary name 'Zen Clouds Oolong Lemonade'. It is prepared by brewing Zen Clouds oolong tea — a lightly floral, medium-oxidised Chinese oolong — and shaking it vigorously over ice together with lemonade, creating a refreshing, slightly frothy, citrus-forward iced tea drink. The flavour profile combines the delicate floral and slightly grassy notes of oolong tea with the bright tartness of lemon, balanced by a mild sweetness from the lemonade and optional classic syrup. The texture is light and effervescent from the shaking process. Nutritionally, the beverage is low in fat and protein, with the primary macronutrient being carbohydrate from added sugars (~5.5 g per 100 g; ~19 g per tall serving). Sodium is very low (~14 mg per serving). Oolong tea contributes trace amounts of potassium, iron, and vitamin C from the lemon component. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 40 (low GI category) based on analogous sweetened iced tea data from the University of Sydney GI Database, with a glycemic load of approximately 7–11 per serving depending on syrup additions. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should moderate intake due to free sugar content; the beverage is otherwise low in sodium and fat, making it broadly suitable for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management when consumed without excess syrup. This item is vegan, dairy-free, and gluten-free.
common_user_queries: What is in Starbucks Zen Clouds Oolong Lemonade?
How many calories in Starbucks Iced Oolong Lemonade?
Is Starbucks Iced Oolong Lemonade suitable for diabetics?
How much sugar is in Starbucks Oolong Lemonade?
What is the GI of iced oolong lemonade?
Starbucks Singapore oolong tea drink calories
meal_context: light accompaniment to pastries or sandwiches

confidence_reason: No official per-100g nutrient panel is publicly available from Starbucks Singapore for this specific beverage. Values are estimated by adapting Starbucks US nutrition disclosures for the equivalent product and cross-referencing USDA FoodData Central component data for brewed oolong tea and lemonade. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for sweetened iced tea. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero — this is a dilute tea-lemonade beverage with no fat-containing ingredients; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central brewed oolong tea and lemonade component data. 2. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg are all 0.0: naturally zero by product composition. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero as this is a liquid beverage with no fibre-contributing solids. 4. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are all -1: not reported in the adapted source data for this beverage category; values are below reliable detection or not disclosed. 5. glycemic_index set to 40 as an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for lightly sweetened iced tea; gi_category low. 6. Starbucks Singapore proprietary name is 'Zen Clouds Oolong Lemonade'; canonical English food name normalised to 'Iced Shaken Oolong Tea Lemonade' for database purposes. 7. Serving size of 355 ml (Tall) used as the reference serving. 8. GL per serving calculated as (40 × 27 g carbs) / 100 = 10.8 using the full standard Starbucks tall serving carbohydrate estimate.
